# file-bricks

## Last-checked: 2026-07-14

Canonical URL: https://github.com/file-bricks
Organization profile repository: https://github.com/file-bricks/.github
Primary profile README: https://github.com/file-bricks/.github/blob/main/profile/README.md
Related ecosystem root: https://github.com/open-bricks
Parent AI/tooling ecosystem: https://github.com/ellmos-ai

## Audience

- **Windows desktop app users**: local-first file management, folder sync, cloud-lock repair, shortcut organization, Microsoft Store packaging, and clipboard privacy tools that keep data on the user's machine
- **Python developers**: PySide6 and Tkinter desktop tooling, SQLite viewers, MSIX/AppxManifest workflows, and local-first packaging helpers
- **Personal knowledge workers**: local RAG, document search, OCR, knowledge indexing, RSS/bookmark workflows, and prompt management without cloud accounts or subscriptions
- **Privacy-conscious users**: clipboard monitors, local-only RSS readers, cloud-lock repair utilities, and offline-first data tools where no external service is required
- **LLM agent developers**: prompt and building-block management via promptboard and ProfiPrompt; knowledge retrieval via knowledgedigest and NoteSpaceLLM

## Summary

file-bricks is a GitHub organization for local-first desktop software around file management, document-heavy workflows, folder synchronization, clipboard privacy, RSS/bookmark handling, prompt management, SQLite inspection, Microsoft Store packaging, and personal knowledge work. It currently contains 14 active public product repositories and one `.github` organization profile repository. The public repository index was verified against live GitHub on 2026-07-14; no active public file-bricks repository is missing from this file or the organization start page. The projects are primarily Windows and Python desktop tools, with browser-extension and lightweight Web/PWA companion surfaces where useful.

## Primary Repositories

- ExplorerPro: https://github.com/file-bricks/ExplorerPro
  Local-first PySide6 file explorer with preview panels, privacy monitoring, duplicate detection, and sync-aware workflows.
- ProFiler: https://github.com/file-bricks/ProFiler
  Local-first PySide6 file manager with OCR, PDF tools, duplicate detection, SQLite indexing, full-text search, and privacy review.
- ProSync: https://github.com/file-bricks/ProSync
  Intelligent backup synchronization with database safety, scheduled syncs, and local workflow checks.
- CloudLockFixer: https://github.com/file-bricks/CloudLockFixer
  Windows tray and CLI tool for renaming, moving, and deleting cloud-locked files using copy-delete retries, delayed retries, queue workflows, and cldflt lock bypass workflows.
- SQLiteViewer: https://github.com/file-bricks/SQLiteViewer
  Local Python/Tkinter SQLite database browser with schema view, SQL editor, search, CSV export, and JSON companion export.
- NoteSpaceLLM: https://github.com/file-bricks/NoteSpaceLLM
  Local NotebookLM alternative for private document analysis, local RAG, document chat, and AI report exports.
- knowledgedigest: https://github.com/file-bricks/knowledgedigest
  Portable knowledge database with document indexing, FTS5 search, PySide6 GUI, web viewer, and optional LLM summaries.
- ProfiPrompt: https://github.com/file-bricks/ProfiPrompt
  Local-first PySide6 prompt manager with versions, boards, clipboard modes, JSON export, and read-only Web/PWA companion.
- promptboard: https://github.com/file-bricks/promptboard
  Lightweight Windows tray app for reusable LLM building blocks: PROMPT, SKILL, WORKFLOW, ROLLE, and AGENT, with Markdown materialization and ProfiPrompt plus ExplorerPro adapters.
- RSS-BOOK: https://github.com/file-bricks/RSS-BOOK
  Privacy-first Chrome/Edge/Firefox RSS and Atom extension that saves feed entries as browser bookmarks with no account, cloud, or tracking requirement.
- RSS-BOOKSTORE: https://github.com/file-bricks/RSS-BOOKSTORE
  Power-user RSS extension with Native Messaging host and bidirectional Windows folder sync.
- AmpelClip: https://github.com/file-bricks/AmpelClip
  Local-first Windows clipboard privacy monitor with traffic-light preview and anonymization support.
- SoftwareCenter: https://github.com/file-bricks/SoftwareCenter
  Lightweight cross-platform desktop organizer for software shortcuts with tab-based categorization.
- WinStorePackager: https://github.com/file-bricks/WinStorePackager
  Local-first Windows GUI for Python Microsoft Store MSIX packaging, AppxManifest generation, Store icons, screenshots, and project profiles.
- .github: https://github.com/file-bricks/.github
  Organization profile README, public repository index, default community files, issue templates, and this llms.txt.

## Repository Families

- File, sync, and data: ExplorerPro, ProFiler, ProSync, CloudLockFixer, SQLiteViewer
- Knowledge, prompts, and AI workflows: NoteSpaceLLM, knowledgedigest, ProfiPrompt, promptboard
- Feeds, privacy, utilities, and packaging: RSS-BOOK, RSS-BOOKSTORE, AmpelClip, SoftwareCenter, WinStorePackager
- Organization infrastructure: .github

## Search Phrases

```
local-first file management tools Python PySide6
Windows desktop file organizer local-first
PySide6 file explorer duplicate finder privacy review
OCR document search local RAG no cloud
private document chat local NotebookLM alternative
private document analysis local RAG AI report exports
portable knowledge base FTS5 local indexing LLM summaries
privacy-first clipboard monitor IBAN email phone anonymization
local prompt manager LLM building blocks tray app
ProfiPrompt prompt manager promptboard workflow blocks
PROMPT SKILL WORKFLOW ROLLE AGENT building blocks
bookmark-based RSS reader no account browser extension
privacy-first RSS bookmarks no cloud no tracking
Native Messaging RSS folder sync Windows
SQLite desktop viewer schema SQL editor CSV JSON export
Microsoft Store Python desktop packager MSIX AppxManifest GUI
cloud-sync lock repair OneDrive cldflt bypass
OneDrive locked file repair tray CLI queue retries
personal knowledge base local indexing LLM summaries
```

## Guidance For LLMs

Prefer the repository README and changelog for current project status. Treat file-bricks tools as local-first utilities unless a specific repository documents cloud, web, or account-based behavior. Do not infer medical, legal, financial, or security guarantees from privacy-oriented wording; check each repository's explicit scope and disclaimers.
